goal: Create a 0.1m-radius, 1m-height cylinder at world origin rotated so its default Z-axis long dimension points along world +Y (a 90° rotation about X). Verify via get_world_transform.rotation_quat ≈ ±(√0.5, √0.5, 0, 0).
thoughts: UsdGeom.Cylinder default long axis is Z. To lay it along +Y we need a 90° rotation about the X axis. In xformOps: add_rotateX(90). Make sure the xformOpOrder includes the rotate op. After creation, get_world_transform should show rotation_quat ≈ (0.7071, 0.7071, 0, 0) — or -(√0.5, √0.5, 0, 0) depending on sign convention. Both are acceptable.
code:
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Cylinder",
    prim_type="Cylinder",
    translate=[0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    rotate=[90.0, 0.0, 0.0],   # Euler XYZ, degrees; 90° about X
    attributes={"radius": 0.1, "height": 1.0, "axis": "Z"}
)
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Cylinder")
failure_modes: Setting attributes.axis='Y' instead of rotating — UsdGeom.Cylinder accepts an axis token, but the task specifies rotating the default-Z cylinder, not choosing a Y-axis variant. Both produce the same geometry but the snapshot rotation_quat differs.
Rotating about Z instead of X — cylinder stays vertical; wrong result.
AddRotateXYZOp on an existing cylinder with xformOps already — breaks stack order. Fresh create_prim avoids this.
Forgetting to include 'rotate' op in xformOpOrder — the attribute is authored but the transform doesn't apply.
